stories.title: EX DeFi Introduces AI-Powered Trading Technology Amid Growing Adoption of Artificial Intelligence in Financial Markets
stories.simple_summary: A company called EX DeFi is using AI to help people trade money and investments automatically. More financial companies are starting to use AI tools like this.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people who trade stocks — if you have a regular job and savings account, this doesn't change anything you need to do.

stories.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets - ABC News
stories.source: Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI (the company that makes ChatGPT) saying that OpenAI stole Apple's private company secrets.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal fight between two big tech companies — nothing you need to act on, but it shows tensions are rising between major AI makers.

stories.title: University of Chicago cutting use of AI by banning technology in classrooms for first-year law students
stories.simple_summary: University of Chicago is telling first-year law students they can't use AI tools in their classes.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a decision at one university — if you're not a student there, it doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook and Instagram's owner) turned off an AI feature on Instagram that people were upset about. The feature let users create fake AI pictures using other people's public photos.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Instagram, you no longer have to worry about this feature — Meta listened to complaints and removed it.

stories.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
stories.simple_summary: Meta is shutting down an Instagram AI tool that made deepfake pictures (fake images that look real) by copying public Instagram accounts without permission.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have a public Instagram account, you're now protected from people using your photos without your say-so to create AI fakes.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft in blockbuster case
stories.simple_summary: Apple is taking OpenAI to court, claiming OpenAI stole Apple's secret technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal battle between two big companies — nothing you need to worry about in your daily life.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, saying OpenAI employees took confidential Apple information.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a major dispute between tech giants — it doesn't affect everyday people or small business owners right now.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI and another company, saying OpenAI's workers (who used to work at Apple) stole Apple's hardware secrets to help build AI devices.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal case between tech companies — nothing you need to do, but it shows Apple is worried about OpenAI copying its ideas.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
stories.simple_summary: Apple has sued OpenAI, saying the company's leaders deliberately directed employees to steal Apple's private secrets.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a lawsuit between two tech companies — it doesn't affect regular people or small business owners.

stories.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
stories.simple_summary: Hugging Face (a company where AI builders share tools and code freely) is becoming more popular. About half of the biggest 500 companies now use their shared AI tools.
stories.why_it_matters: More companies are choosing to build their own AI instead of renting it from big companies — this could mean cheaper AI tools available in the future.

stories.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
stories.simple_summary: SK Hynix, a company that makes computer chips for AI, just raised over $26 billion in the stock market. The U.S. government is asking them to build factories here.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and chip makers — it shows the U.S. wants to make more AI computer chips at home instead of buying them from other countries.

stories.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
stories.simple_summary: Hugging Face's leader says more big companies are building their own AI tools instead of paying to rent AI from other companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This could mean companies have more control over their AI and costs might come down over time, but it mostly affects large businesses right now.
